text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise owl who lived high up in an aged oak tree. Every morning, the owl would sweep branches beneath her with soft fluttering wings. One day, she noticed a young hare studying maps.

"Why do you study stars?" quizzed the owl.

"The stars guide me home," replied the hare.

"Well," chirped the owl, "I find stars guide me to adventures."

The hare listened intently. One rainy night, the owl told stories under the glow of moonlight.

They talked of mysterious islands where oceans parted. Of shy deer that danced under the moonlight. Of feathers as precious jewels.

Soon, the hare couldn't wait.

First hunt. Then feather ornaments.

One after midnight. They sailed.

Days sailed by. Night after night.

Stars wondrous. Islands grand. Deer didn't dart.

Found paradise.

Years passed.

Stars didn't fade.

She had treasures.

Now an owl lady.

Golden feathers.

Her own map.
